blocks.content: He sat on the edge of the bed.
He sat there for a moment that had no length — not a second, not a minute. Something outside of time entirely.
Still. The way a face is still when the person who lived in it has left. He had never seen it before. He had hoped he would never see it. He recognized it immediately.

He had always known this day existed. They both had. They had chosen not to live in fear of it and they had succeeded — fifteen years of not living in fear of it, fifteen years of just living — and still, now that it had arrived, it was the most unexpected thing in the world.
He said her name once more, into the room.
And then everything came at once.
